title: Neighbors, safety expert say abandoned backyard pool owned by Micron is dangerous
summary: Neighbors and safety experts are raising concerns about an abandoned residential pool on a Henry Clay Boulevard property owned by Micron Technology. The property was purchased nearly three years ago by the company for a planned industrial wastewater line project, but the pool presents an apparent public safety hazard requiring municipal attention.

political_signal.description: Community members escalating concerns about corporate property maintenance standards and municipal enforcement of safety codes on industrial company holdings within residential areas.

processing_notes: Article focuses on property safety hazard rather than Micron's primary development project
No dollar amounts or specific timeline for wastewater line project mentioned
May relate to broader code-enforcement or permitting processes for stalled commercial projects in residential zones
Consider cross-referencing with 311 service requests and GIS mapping of Micron holdings in Syracuse
